q: Where is the underwater lab located in IIT Jammu?
a: The Underwater Research Lab (also known as the Underwater Artificial Intelligence Lab) at IIT Jammu is located at the Jagti Campus (near the SBI ATM). It houses a unique 25ft x 20ft underwater acoustic test facility simulating oceanic conditions including waves and noise. The research focuses on underwater robotics, autonomous systems, surveillance, and signal processing. The lab is headed by Dr. Karan Nathwani (Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering) along with Dr. Badri Narayan Subudhi (Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering) and Dr. Ankur Bansal (Assistant Professor, Electrical Engineering). Source: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/ee/index.html

q: What is the BTech fee at IIT Jammu?
a: For the B.Tech 2025 batch in academic year 2025-26, the official fee PDF lists tuition per semester as Rs. 1,00,000 for General/OBC/EWS students with parental income above Rs. 5 lakh, Rs. 33,333 for income between Rs. 1 lakh and Rs. 5 lakh, and zero tuition for income below Rs. 1 lakh. SC/ST/PH students have zero tuition fee. The same PDF lists semester fee of Rs. 35,480 for single occupancy and Rs. 30,370 for shared occupancy, plus one-time and security fees at admission. Mess charges are notified separately. Source: IIT Jammu B.Tech 2025-26 fee PDF

q: What hostel facilities are available at IIT Jammu?
a: IIT Jammu has separate hostels for boys and girls, with 9 Boys' hostels (including Canary Hostel, Braeg Hostel, and Fulgar Hostel) and 2 Girls' hostels (including Dedhar Hostel and Egret Hostel) at the Jagti campus. Hostel facilities include WiFi, laundry centers, reading rooms, common halls, recreation areas, indoor games facilities, water coolers, and washing machines. Rooms are generally twin-sharing, equipped with basic furniture (bed, table, chair, wardrobe, bookshelf, book rack). Additionally, there is a New Hostel Block, which is an eight-storey centrally air-conditioned building providing 662 single-seater rooms. A four-storey dining hall is also available that can accommodate approximately 480 students at a time. Mattresses, pillows, buckets, etc. must be brought by the student or purchased locally. Prohibited items include water heaters, irons, electric stoves, and table fans. Source: IIT Jammu Hostels & Facilities FAQ

q: What is the mess fee at IIT Jammu?
a: IIT Jammu features multiple dining options including the Canary Mess, Dedhar Mess, Egret Mess, and Annapurna Mess, serving vegetarian and non-vegetarian meals. The mess menu is managed by a student committee with mess charges around Rs. 3,200 to 3,500 per month (notified as a mess advance). Food & utility facilities on campus include Café Coffee Day (CCD) and Nescafe outlets, multiple academic canteens, and a night canteen operating until midnight. For conveniences, a daily utility store and stationery facilities are available inside the campus. Banking facilities (including an SBI ATM) are also accessible on campus. Source: IIT Jammu Mess & Dining Circular

q: how many blocks are in iit?
a: The permanent IIT Jammu main campus is located at Jagti on NH-44, Nagrota, spread across 400 acres with Phase 1A operational. The primary buildings/blocks include Pushkar Block (Academic Block), Mansar Block (Lecture Hall Block), and the Paloura Campus (the original/old campus). An SBI ATM is located near the campus entrance. Source: IIT Jammu Infrastructure & Permanent Campus Page

q: What is Pushkar building?
a:
Pushkar Block (Academic Block) is a major eight-story engineering academic block at the IIT Jammu Jagti campus serving as a hub for modern academic and research infrastructure. The building contains 10 classrooms (studio-like, fully equipped spaces for undergraduate and postgraduate lectures), 52 laboratories (including 25 engineering laboratories), 104 faculty cabins/offices, seminar halls (such as Seminar Hall 11AC3027 and Seminar Hall-I), student discussion areas, WiFi-enabled infrastructure, and Innovation Centers for STEM outreach programs, hackathons, and entrepreneurship workshops. It supports departmental teaching, labs, and research activities. The departments associated with Pushkar Block include:
- **Mechanical Engineering**: Founding department, located in Pushkar Block, containing mechanical labs, workshops, classrooms, and offices. Research includes thermal engineering, robotics, manufacturing, materials, and design.
- **Computer Science and Engineering (CSE)**: Classes, computer labs, AI/ML research, coding infrastructure, and project spaces are hosted here.
- **Electrical Engineering (EE)**: Houses electrical labs, electronics systems labs, signal processing, embedded systems, and power systems facilities.

q: What is Mansar building?
a: Mansar Block / Building (Lecture Hall Block) at IIT Jammu is a major three-story academic facility located within the permanent Jagti campus. It primarily serves as a central hub for student learning and institutional events. It houses modern multi-story lecture rooms/classrooms, the Central Library, and the Mansar Auditorium (a 300-seat auditorium used for conclaves, talks, and events). The total lecture capacity of Mansar Block exceeds 2,500 students. Source: IIT Jammu Infrastructure & Permanent Campus Page

q: what general facilities are available on campus?
a:
IIT Jammu Jagti campus features premium utilities and facilities:
- **Internet & WiFi**: The entire campus is WiFi-enabled with 1 Gbps internet connectivity, managed by the Central Computing and Communication Infrastructure (C3I).
- **Smart Classrooms**: Modern digital classrooms with advanced teaching systems and facilities.
- **High Performance Computing (HPC)**: The 'Agastya' HPC facility supports advanced computing and research.
- **Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF)**: The 'Saptarshi' facility houses advanced research instruments.
- **Medical Services**: A medical center inside campus provides 24x7 paramedic support, regular visits by senior doctors, and medical insurance for all students.
- **Security**: Centrally secured campus with smart CCTV surveillance, security staff, and deployment control systems.
- **Banking**: Banking facilities and an SBI ATM are available on campus. Source: IIT Jammu General Campus Facilities & C3I

q: who are the phd students under badri narayan subudhi?
a:
IIT Jammu maintains structured lists of PhD scholars and their supervisors. Examples of research group supervision include:
- **Dr. Badri Narayan Subudhi (Associate Professor, EE)**: Supervises Alisha Gupta (co-supervised, heart rate detection), Gokul Singh Chauhan (medical image analysis), Himanshu Singh (co-supervised, action recognition), Mahima Gandotra (co-supervised, grid-connected renewable energy), Meghna (co-supervised, underwater surveillance), Mehvish Nissar (underwater surveillance), Sarif Saleem (deep-sea video surveillance), and Zareena Amin (co-supervised, DeepFake detection).
- **Dr. Karan Nathwani (Associate Professor, EE)**: Supervises Murtiza Ali (underwater and aerial acoustics), Pawan Kumar, Rantu Buragohain (co-supervised, EEG signal classification), Ritujoy Biswas, Tarun Bali, and Zareena Amin (co-supervised, DeepFake detection).
- **Other Faculty**: Dr. Harpreet Kour and Dr. Harleen Kour are not registered faculty members at IIT Jammu; there are no faculty members by those names. (Please check Harkeerat Kaur for CSE-related biometrics/blockchains).Source: IIT Jammu PhD Scholars & Research Directory

q: which professor working on ml?
a:
IIT Jammu faculty members work on cutting-edge research topics across engineering and sciences:
- **Machine Learning & Deep Learning**: Dr. Yamuna Prasad, Dr. Karan Nathwani, Dr. Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee, Dr. Harkeerat Kaur, Dr. Vinit Jakhetiya, Dr. Badri Narayan Subudhi, and Dr. Divyesh Varade.

q: What is the GATE cutoff for CSE at IIT Jammu?
a: GATE cutoff scores and shortlisting requirements for M.Tech admissions at IIT Jammu vary each year depending on the number of applicants, vacancies, and department-specific criteria. Numerical GATE cutoffs are not fixed. For details on shortlists and criteria, check the PG Admissions portal at https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/Programme/pgadmissions or contact the Post Graduate Office at pgoffice.acad@iitjammu.ac.in. Note: JEE Advanced cutoff ranks (e.g. top 4000-5000) are only for undergraduate B.Tech admissions and do not apply to M.Tech admissions. Source: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/Programme/pgadmissions

q: what are the cutoff marks for BTech admission?
a: Cutoff ranks for B.Tech admission at IIT Jammu are based on JEE Advanced ranks and are determined during JoSAA counseling. Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) is the most sought-after branch, typically closing within the top 4000-5000 ranks in JEE Advanced. Cutoffs for other branches like Civil, Chemical, or Materials Engineering can go higher (e.g., around 10,000 to 15,000+ depending on category). For the most up-to-date and batch-specific cutoff ranks, please visit JoSAA or the official IIT Jammu UG admissions page. Source: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/Programme/ugadmissions

q: Tell me about Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu.

Official pages say it offers B.Tech., M.Tech. and Ph.D. programmes, with labs such as Fluid Mechanics, Control Engineering, Kinematics & Dynamics of Machine, Heat & Mass Transfer, Energy Systems, Solid Mechanics and Manufacturing. q: What are the research interests of Prof. Abhay Sharma?
a: Prof. Abhay Sharma is listed as Professor in Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. Email: abhay.sharma@iitjammu.ac.in. q: What are the research interests of Dr. Aditya Shankar Sandupatla?
a: Dr. Aditya Shankar Sandupatla is listed as Assistant Professor in Chemical Engineering at IIT Jammu. Their office is located in Room 11AC5036 on the 5th Floor (Level L5) of the 11AC Academic Block (Pushkar Block). Email: aditya.sandupatla@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Research Areas 1. Atomistic and molecular modelling of materials, physical phenomenon and chemical reactions using following methods: Density Functional Theory (DFT), Molecular Dynamics (MD), and Kinetic Monte Carlo (KMC) simulations 2. Computational and experimental investigation of heterogeneous catalysts and catalysis of industrial importance Research Interests Computational and experimental investigation of: • Activation of methane to produce hydrogen and carbon nanotubes • Catalytic dehydrogenation of propane to produce hydrogen and propene • Atomic Layer Deposition of materials Teaching . Selected publications are listed on the official profile; sample: 1. A.S. Sandupatla , and A. Chatterjee, Nanoporosity Evolution During Dealloying: Interplay Between Chemical Dissolution, Material Defects, Coarsening and Local Structural Rearrangements over Long Timescales, Acta Materialia , 2021, 213, 116974 (1-13). 2. K. Ray*, A.S. Sandupatla *, and G. Deo, Activity and stability descriptors of Ni based alloy c. Source: https://iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/adityashankarsandupatla

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Ajay Gautam?
a: Dr. Ajay Gautam is listed as Assistant Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jammu. Their office is located in Room 11AC5033 on the 5th Floor (Level L5) of the 11AC Academic Block (Pushkar Block). Email: ajay.gautam@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Development of solid electrolytes Solid-state batteries Structure–transport correlations in energy storage materials Materials characterization (X-ray and neutron diffraction) Battery materials processing Carbon-based materials for gas sensing, energy conversion, and storage applications.. q: What are the research interests of Dr. Garima Singh?
a: Dr. Garima Singh is listed as Assistant Professor in Humanities & Social Sciences at IIT Jammu. Email: garima.singh@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Dr. Garima Singh’s academic interests lie at the intersection of cognitive neuroscience and psychology, with a particular focus on the neural mechanisms underlying reward processing, decision-making, and aberrant behaviors in clinical versus healthy populations. Using advanced neuroimaging methods, including EEG, her work aims to deepen our understanding of how neural pathways impact cognitive and behavioral health, especially in relation to psychiatric conditions affecting the reward system. In addition to these areas, Dr. Singh pursues separate research in delay discounting, metacognition, a. Selected publications are listed on the official profile; sample: 1. Singh, G., Campbell, E., Hogeveen, J., Witkiewitz, K., Claus, E.D. & Cavanagh, J.F. (2023) Alcohol Imagery Boosts the Reward Related Delta Power in Hazardous Drinkers. Psychiatry Research: Neuroimaging 2. Campbell, E., Singh, G., Hogeveen, J., Claus, E., Witkiewitz, K., Cavanagh, J., (2023). Electrophysiological markers of aberrant cue-specific . Source: https://iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/garimasingh

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Mithu Baidya?
a: Dr. Mithu Baidya is listed as Assistant Professor in Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu. Email: mithu.baidya@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Research Areas Area: G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) biology and Cell signaling Approaches: Biochemical, biophysical and chemogenetic based methods. Research Interests G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling. GPCRs are the largest receptor superfamily in the human genome, and it plays indispensable roles in almost all the known physiological processes. As a result, almost half of the currently prescribed drugs work by targeting GPCRs. Our lab’s long-term focus is to understand the underlying molecular basis of GP. Selected publications are listed on the official profile; sample: Selected Publications Breaking the one-site myth: the multifaceted world of proton sensing in GPCRs. Agrawal M, Singh, SK, Baidya M # and Kumari P # . FEBS Journal (2025). pp1-14. http://doi.org/10.1111/febs.70145 Exploring Bias in GPCR Signaling and its Implication in Drug Development: A One-Sided Affair. Borah MP, Trakroo D, Kumari P # , Baidya M. Source: https://iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/mithubaidya

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Santhilal Subhash?
a: Dr. Santhilal Subhash is listed as Assistant Professor in Inter Disciplinary Program, Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu. Email: santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Our goal is to integrate high-dimensional data from genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics to predict how regulatory elements—such as enhancers, promoters, and long noncoding RNAs—govern gene expression in specific tissue contexts. To accomplish this, our lab is focusing on exploring the following research areas. Identifying tissue-specific regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches. Using Single Cell Multiomic Techniques to Understand Complex Regulation of Female Reproductive Tissues. Regulome Trained Computational Models to Predict Female Reproductive H. Sperm Originated Chromatin Imprints and LincRNAs in Organismal Development and Can. Source: https://iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/santhilalsubhash

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Shanmugadas K.P?
a: Dr. Shanmugadas K.P is listed as Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. q: What are the research interests of Dr. Shantanu Vijay Madge?
a: Dr. Shantanu Vijay Madge is listed as Associate Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jammu. Their office is located in Room 11AC5030 on the 5th Floor (Level L5) of the 11AC Academic Block (Pushkar Block). Email: shantanu.madge@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Physical Metallurgy, Mechanical Metallurgy, Materials Processing, Wear and Surface Engineering (Tribology). Metal 3D Printing (Additive Manufacturing), Cold Spray Deposition, Rapid Solidification, Amorphous Alloys, High Entropy Alloys. Electron Microscopy (SEM/EBSD/TEM) and X-ray DIffraction of Materials.. Kawashima, A. L. Greer, A. Inoue . Mater and Design 2016, 101, p. 146. 2. "Novel W-based metallic glass with high hardness and wear resistance". S. V. Madge, A. Caron, R. Gralla, G. Wilde, S. K. Mishra. Intermetallics. Source: https://iitjammu.ac.in/faculty/shantanuvmadge

Their office is located in Room 11AC7011 on the 7th Floor (Level L7) of the 11AC Academic Block (Pushkar Block). Email: srishilan.c@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: 1. q: What are the research interests of Dr. Sudhakar Modem?

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Suman Sarkar?

q: What are the research interests of Dr. Tanmay Sarkar?
a: Dr. Tanmay Sarkar is listed as Associate Professor in Mathematics at IIT Jammu. Email: tanmay.sarkar@iitjammu.ac.in. Research/academic interests: Research Area: Partial Differential Equations and Numerics. More precisely, I am interested in hyperbolic conservation laws and numerics, nonlinear dispersive equations, degenerate convection-diffusion equations, discontinuous Galerkin method for evolution equations, optimal control and inverse problems related to evolution equations. Teaching Interests: Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Real and Functional Analysis..
